steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
To expand $(7x^3 - 2) - (-5x^3 + 9)$, distribute the subtraction across the second parentheses. Each term inside changes sign:
$$7x^3 - 2 - (-5x^3) - 9$$
Which becomes:
$$7x^3 - 2 + 5x^3 - 9$$

steps.title: Combine like terms to find b
steps.content:
Collect the $x^3$ terms together and the constants together:
$$(7x^3 + 5x^3) + (-2 - 9) = 12x^3 - 11$$
The simplified expression matches the form $bx^3 - 11$, so $b = 12$.
